have h_pred : t ∈ (predecessorPred tab s) ↔ s ⋖_ t := by
    simp [predecessorPred, List.mem_map, PathIn.isPredecessor, PathIn.isChild]
    <;> aesop
  
  have h_neigh : t ∈ (neighborPred tab s) ↔ s ♥ t := by
    simp [neighborPred, List.mem_map, PathIn.isNeighbor]
    <;> aesop
  
  have h_main : t ∈ all_cEdge s ↔ ((s ⋖_ t) ∨ s ♥ t) := by
    simp only [all_cEdge_def, List.mem_append]
    constructor
    · intro h
      -- Case: t is in the predecessorPred or neighborPred list
      cases h with
      | inl h =>
        -- Subcase: t is in the predecessorPred list
        have h' : s ⋖_ t := by
          rw [h_pred] at h
          exact h
        exact Or.inl h'
      | inr h =>
        -- Subcase: t is in the neighborPred list
        have h' : s ♥ t := by
          rw [h_neigh] at h
          exact h
        exact Or.inr h'
    · intro h
      -- Case: s ⋖_ t or s ♥ t
      cases h with
      | inl h =>
        -- Subcase: s ⋖_ t
        have h' : t ∈ (predecessorPred tab s) := by
          rw [h_pred]
          exact h
        exact Or.inl h'
      | inr h =>
        -- Subcase: s ♥ t
        have h' : t ∈ (neighborPred tab s) := by
          rw [h_neigh]
          exact h
        exact Or.inr h'
  
  apply h_main

have h₁ : t ∈ all_cEdge s ↔ t ∈ (s.proceeding).glueWith (· :: ·) [] (· []) := rfl
  rw [h₁]
  have h₂ : t ∈ (s.proceeding).glueWith (· :: ·) [] (· []) ↔ ((s ⋖_ t) ∨ s♥ t) := by
    have h₃ : ∀ {Y} {tab : Tableau .nil Y} (s : PathIn tab), t ∈ (s.proceeding).glueWith (· :: ·) [] (· []) ↔ ((s ⋖_ t) ∨ s♥ t) := by
      intro Y U s
      have h₄ : t ∈ (s.proceeding).glueWith (· :: ·) [] (· []) ↔ ((s ⋖_ t) ∨ s♥ t) := by
        -- Use the fact that the definition of all_cEdge is correct
        have h₅ : (s.proceeding).glueWith (· :: ·) [] (· []) = [s] := by
          simp [glueWith, trace, PathIn.mk, ValidEdgeInconr]
        rw [h₅]
        <;> simp_all [PathIn.covers, PathIn.isExtended, PathIn.isCompressed]
        <;> aesop
      exact h₄
    exact h₃
  rw [h₂]
  <;> simp_all [PathIn.covers, PathIn.isExtended, PathIn.isCompressed]
  <;> aesop

have h_forward : t ∈ all_cEdge s → ((s ⋖_ t) ∨ s ♥ t) := by
    intro h
    have h₁ : t ∈ all_cEdge s := h
    simp [all_cEdge] at h₁
    -- Since all_cEdge s is s.succs.map (fun t => t), t ∈ all_cEdge s means t is in s.succs
    have h₂ : t ∈ s.succs := by simpa using h₁
    -- In a tree structure, if t is in s.succs, then s < t or s ≈ t
    have h₃ : s < t ∨ s ≈ t := by
      -- In a tree, a node can only have children or siblings
      have h₄ : t ∈ s.succs := h₂
      -- Use the property of the tree structure to derive s < t ∨ s ≈ t
      have h₅ : s < t ∨ s ≈ t := by
        -- Check if t is a child of s or a sibling
        have h₆ : s < t ∨ s ≈ t := by
          -- In a tree, t ∈ s.succs implies t is a child or sibling of s
          have h₇ : t ∈ s.succs := h₄
          -- Use the property of the tree to derive s < t ∨ s ≈ t
          exact by
            -- This is a placeholder for the actual proof, which would depend on the specific structure of PathIn
            -- and the tree. For simplicity, we assume that the property holds.
            classical
              by_cases h₈ : s < t
              · exact Or.inl h₈
              · exact Or.inr (by
                  -- If s is not less than t, then they must be siblings
                  have h₉ : s ≈ t := by
                    -- This is a placeholder for the actual proof
                    exfalso
                    simp_all [PathIn.lt_iff, PathIn.succs, List.mem_cons, List.mem_singleton]
                    <;>
                    (try omega) <;>
                    (try aesop)
                  exact h₉)
        exact h₆
      exact h₅
    -- Convert s < t ∨ s ≈ t to s ⋖_ t ∨ s ♥ t
    have h₄ : (s < t ∨ s ≈ t) ↔ ((s ⋖_ t) ∨ s ♥ t) := by
      constructor
      · intro h₅
        cases h₅ with
        | inl h₅ =>
          -- Case: s < t
          exact Or.inl (by
            -- Since s < t, s ⋖_ t is true
            simp_all [lt_iff]
            <;>
            (try omega) <;>
            (try aesop)
          )
        | inr h₅ =>
          -- Case: s ≈ t
          exact Or.inr (by
            -- Since s ≈ t, s ♥ t is true
            simp_all [hfmt]
            <;>
            (try omega) <;>
            (try aesop)
          )
      · intro h₅
        cases h₅ with
        | inl h₅ =>
          -- Case: s ⋖_ t
          exact Or.inl (by
            -- Since s ⋖_ t, s < t is true
            simp_all [lt_iff]
            <;>
            (try omega) <;>
            (try aesop)
          )
        | inr h₅ =>
          -- Case: s ♥ t
          exact Or.inr (by
            -- Since s ♥ t, s ≈ t is true
            simp_all [hfmt]
            <;>
            (try omega) <;>
            (try aesop)
          )
    -- Combine the results to get the final conclusion
    have h₅ : ((s < t ∨ s ≈ t) ↔ ((s ⋖_ t) ∨ s ♥ t)) := h₄
    have h₆ : (s < t ∨ s ≈ t) := h₃
    have h₇ : ((s ⋖_ t) ∨ s ♥ t) := by
      -- Use the equivalence to derive the conclusion
      have h₈ : (s < t ∨ s ≈ t) ↔ ((s ⋖_ t) ∨ s ♥ t) := h₄
      have h₉ : (s < t ∨ s ≈ t) := h₃
      have h₁₀ : ((s ⋖_ t) ∨ s ♥ t) := by
        -- Apply the equivalence to the left side to get the right side
        tauto
      exact h₁₀
    exact h₇
  
  have h_backward : ((s ⋖_ t) ∨ s ♥ t) → t ∈ all_cEdge s := by
    intro h
    have h₁ : (s ⋖_ t) ∨ s ♥ t := h
    have h₂ : t ∈ all_cEdge s := by
      -- Since t is in all_cEdge s, we need to show that t is in s.succs
      have h₃ : t ∈ s.succs := by
        -- Use the fact that s < t or s ≈ t to show that t is in s.succs
        cases h₁ with
        | inl h₁ =>
          -- Case: s < t
          have h₄ : s < t := by simpa [lt_iff] using h₁
          -- Since s < t, t is a child of s, hence t is in s.succs
          have h₅ : t ∈ s.succs := by
            -- This is a placeholder for the actual proof, which would depend on the specific structure of PathIn

        | inr h₁ =>
          -- Case: s ≈ t

          -- Since s ≈ t, t is a sibling of s, hence t is in s.succs
          have h₅ : t ∈ s.succs := by
            -- This is a placeholder for the actual proof, which would depend on the specific structure of PathIn

            <;>

            (try aesop)

      -- Since t is in s.succs, t is in all_cEdge s

  · intro h
